summary: Observed Explorer runtime setting for protected operating system file visibility. Microsoft Open Specifications documents ShowSuperHidden under Explorer\Advanced with 1 = enable and 0 = disable, the 25H2 dump/default hive shows the same value on current builds, and a reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 confirmed that Explorer.EXE queries HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowSuperHidden with both Data:0 and Data:1 after Explorer restart.

setting.name: Show Protected Operating System Files
setting.casual_explanation: File Explorer can hide or reveal protected operating system files. Most people should leave them hidden, but advanced troubleshooting sometimes requires seeing them.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change this when they need to inspect system-owned files during troubleshooting, reverse engineering, or shell behavior testing.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Showing protected operating system files exposes sensitive system content that Explorer normally keeps hidden. It is a low-risk user preference technically, but it is not recommended for general users because it makes accidental edits easier.
setting.professional_notes: Microsoft Open Specifications maps the showSuperHidden folder option to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowSuperHidden and defines 1 as enabled and 0 as disabled. The 25H2 dump corroborates that the same value is present under the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch, and the HKCU25H2 default hive exports ShowSuperHidden = 0. A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon probe on 2026-03-24 then confirmed live Explorer consumption: after writing ShowSuperHidden = 0 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the value with Data: 0; after writing ShowSuperHidden = 1 and restarting Explorer, Explorer.EXE queried the same value with Data: 1. The companion Hidden value was held at 1 during the probe so the protected-file gate was exercised in the 'show hidden items' mode that makes ShowSuperHidden relevant.

allowed_values.label: Hide protected operating system files
allowed_values.meaning: Protected operating system files stay hidden. Microsoft documents 0 as the disabled state, the 25H2 default hive exports ShowSuperHidden = 0, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 0 after restart.

allowed_values.label: Show protected operating system files
allowed_values.meaning: Protected operating system files become visible. Microsoft documents 1 as the enabled state, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture shows Explorer.EXE querying Data: 1 after restart.

targets.notes: This record validates the documented numeric semantics with current 25H2 dump corroboration and a live Explorer runtime trace.

app_current_implementation.notes: The app now writes ShowSuperHidden = 1 on the same current-user Explorer path validated by the Microsoft spec and the Win25H2Clean Procmon capture.

windows_defaults.label: 25H2 default Explorer preference
windows_defaults.applies_to: Current-user Explorer Advanced settings on the 25H2 default hive snapshot

states.rationale: HKCU25H2.reg exports ShowSuperHidden = 0 in the default Explorer\Advanced block.

windows_defaults.notes: This is the current 25H2 default-hive corroboration, not a universal promise for every historical Windows build.

recommended_profiles.label: Hide protected operating system files
intended_for: Systems that should keep Explorer on the safer default
avoid_for: Advanced troubleshooting sessions that require direct system-file visibility

states.rationale: This matches the documented disabled state and the 25H2 default-hive export.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Explorer to keep protected operating system files hidden.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need to inspect protected operating system files directly.
tradeoffs: Reduces the chance of accidental edits to protected files.

recommended_profiles.label: Show protected operating system files
avoid_for: Anyone likely to browse system folders casually

states.rationale: This matches the documented enabled state and the runtime-observed Explorer read.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You intentionally need Explorer to reveal protected operating system files.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want the safer default Explorer presentation.
tradeoffs: Exposes protected operating system files in Explorer.
Helps advanced inspection but makes accidental edits easier.

evidence.title: Microsoft Open Specifications: GlobalFolderOptionsVista showSuperHidden
evidence.summary: Microsoft maps showSuperHidden to HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowSuperHidden and states that it MUST be 1 to enable or 0 to disable. The description says the option displays protected operating system files.

evidence.title: 25H2 dump and default hive corroboration for ShowSuperHidden
evidence.summary: The 25H2 default hive exports ShowSuperHidden = 0 under HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced, and the raw 25H2 registry traces also list the same value name under the current-user Explorer\Advanced branch.

evidence.title: Procmon capture - Explorer ShowSuperHidden runtime surface
evidence.summary: A reversible Win25H2Clean Procmon capture on 2026-03-24 wrote ShowSuperHidden = 0 and ShowSuperHidden = 1 with Hidden held at 1, restarted Explorer after each write, and captured Explorer.EXE querying HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowSuperHidden with matching Data: 0 and Data: 1 values.

decision.why: Microsoft documents the numeric semantics, the 25H2 dump corroborates the current surface and default value, and the Win25H2Clean Procmon trace confirms live Explorer consumption of both 0 and 1 on this build. The setting is still best reserved for advanced users because it exposes protected operating system files.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: STATE=0 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowSuperHidden ... Data: 0; STATE=1 ... Explorer.EXE RegQueryValue HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\Advanced\ShowSuperHidden ... Data: 1; RESTORED_SHOWSUPERHIDDEN_VALUE=1
validation_proof.notes: Raw hit exports were also copied to H:\Temp\vm-tooling-staging\showsuperhidden-0-hits.csv and H:\Temp\vm-tooling-staging\showsuperhidden-1-hits.csv.
